siteSubtitle: An interactive policy intelligence platform for sovereign disaster risk financing and catastrophe bond case studies.
heroDescription: Explore sovereign catastrophe bond programs, country case studies, official documents, presentations, and policy insights through an interactive globe-based platform.

introVideo.title: Platform Introduction Video
introVideo.description: Introductory video shown on the homepage.

generalPresentation.title: Catastrophe Bonds - General Overview Presentation
generalPresentation.description: General presentation introducing catastrophe bonds, sovereign risk transfer, trigger types, and policy relevance.

generalDocument.title: General Information on Catastrophe Bonds
generalDocument.description: General background document on catastrophe bonds, market structure, and sovereign disaster risk financing.

generalSummary: Catastrophe bonds are financial instruments that transfer disaster risk from governments or insurers to capital market investors. They are used to improve fiscal resilience, provide rapid post-disaster liquidity, and diversify sources of disaster risk financing.

policyOverview.title: Policy Overview
policyOverview.summary: This section explains what catastrophe bonds are, how sovereign structures work, why governments use them, and the key advantages and limitations of this instrument.
